ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general green tea beverage class; no specific entry for honey ume green tea exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers green tea as a beverage category. No specific honey ume variant exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34728003 covers green tea as a substance. No specific honey ume green tea concept exists; closest parent used.

preparation_method: blended with honey and ume plum syrup

serving.natural_language_portion: One large cup of Honey Ume Green Tea as served at KOI Thé, approximately 500 ml, containing brewed green tea sweetened with honey and ume plum syrup.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, tea, green, brewed, regular (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 14355) adapted with honey and ume plum syrup addition
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base green tea nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed green tea (FDC ID 171917). Honey contribution estimated from USDA FDC entry for honey (FDC ID 169640). Ume plum syrup contribution estimated from USDA FDC entry for plum juice/syrup analogue. Final per-100g values represent a blended estimate for a sweetened green tea beverage at approximately 8–10 g sugar per 100 ml, consistent with typical Taiwanese bubble tea shop sweetened tea formulations. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 (Atkinson et al.) — honey and sweetened tea analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for KOI Honey Ume Green Tea. GI estimated at 50 based on: (1) plain green tea has a negligible GI (~1); (2) honey has a GI of approximately 55–58 (University of Sydney GI Database); (3) ume plum syrup is a fructose-dominant sweetener with GI approximately 40–50. The blended beverage at ~8.5 g carbohydrate per 100 ml is dominated by honey and plum syrup sugars. A composite GI of 50 (low category) is used as a conservative analogous estimate. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving = (50 × 42.5 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 21.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~15 mg per 500 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 15 mg sodium per large serving, Honey Ume Green Tea poses minimal sodium concern for individuals managing hypertension; however, the added sugar content should be considered in the context of overall dietary pattern and weight management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugars from honey and ume plum syrup contributing approximately 41 g sugar per 500 ml serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Honey Ume Green Tea with caution; a 500 ml large serving contains approximately 41 g of added sugars (honey and ume plum syrup), which can cause a significant postprandial blood glucose rise, and portion reduction or sugar-level customisation is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and saturated fat; green tea polyphenols may confer modest cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Honey Ume Green Tea contains negligible fat and saturated fat, and the green tea base provides catechins and polyphenols associated with modest cardiovascular benefit; however, the high added sugar content warrants moderation to avoid contributing to hypertriglyceridaemia in individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Honey Ume Green Tea (Large / 大-蜂蜜青梅绿茶) is a Taiwanese-style sweetened cold tea beverage served at KOI Thé, a popular Taiwanese tea chain with outlets in Singapore. The drink is made by brewing high-quality green tea and blending it with natural honey and ume (Japanese sour plum) syrup, producing a refreshing, lightly tart, and floral-sweet flavour profile. The ume plum contributes a distinctive fruity sourness that balances the sweetness of the honey, while the green tea base provides a clean, grassy, slightly astringent backdrop. The beverage is typically served chilled or over ice in a large cup of approximately 500 ml. Nutritionally, the drink is low in fat and protein but contains a meaningful quantity of added sugars (approximately 41 g per large serving) derived from honey and ume plum syrup, contributing around 170 kcal per serving. The green tea base provides catechins and polyphenols with antioxidant properties. Clinically, the beverage is of moderate concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its added sugar load, and should be consumed in moderation or with reduced sweetness customisation. Sodium content is very low, making it suitable for individuals managing hypertension. The absence of dairy and gluten makes it suitable for lactose-intolerant and gluten-sensitive individuals. This beverage is a signature item in the Taiwanese bubble tea and specialty tea category widely available across Singapore.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional analysis of KOI Honey Ume Green Tea exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for brewed green tea and honey, with ume plum syrup contribution estimated by analogy. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogues for honey and fructose-dominant sweeteners. Confidence is medium due to the estimation basis.
notes: Fat_g is naturally zero as green tea brewed beverages contain no dietary fat by nature; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171917). Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, dietary_fibre_g, and cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero for this beverage type per USDA FoodData Central. Protein_g is trace (0.1 g per 100 ml) from green tea solids. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this beverage type. Vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI 50 × carbohydrate_g per serving 42.5 g) / 100 = 21.3. The food is classified as Taiwanese cuisine served in Singapore; multilingual names are provided for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean multilingual naming rules applicable to beverages sold in Singapore. The input description 大-蜂蜜青梅绿茶 is preserved in aliases as 大蜂蜜青梅绿茶. Price data from field survey data (SGD 7.70) is noted but not included in the nutritional record as it is not a nutritional field.
